patch.value: pm claim silently steals items already assigned to another agent — claim must be atomic test-and-set for multi-agent work distribution

bypassesAssigneeConflict (src/core/store/item-store.ts) unconditionally exempts op === "claim" from the assignee-conflict check, so `pm claim` on an item assigned to a DIFFERENT author silently reassigns it with no warning and no --force. Empirical: 6 parallel `pm claim` on one item → 3 return success each telling a different agent "you own it"; final assignee is whoever wrote last (claimer-4 while claimer-1 and claimer-3 also got success payloads). Sequential steal also confirmed: claim by second-agent silently overwrites first-agent. This defeats claim as a work-distribution primitive: two agents proceed believing they own the same task. Fix: make claim test-and-set under the per-item lock — if assignee is set, non-empty, and !== author, fail with structured `already_claimed_by` (exit CONFLICT, include current owner + claimed timestamp) unless `--steal`/`--force`; claiming an item you already own stays idempotent-success. Consider companion `pm claim --next` (atomic pm next recommendation + claim, retry-on-conflict with next candidate) so N parallel agents each get a distinct item instead of all claiming the same `pm next` recommendation.

pm claim of an item assigned to another agent fails with exit-code CONFLICT and code already_claimed_by unless --force; --if-available still skips as no-op; empty-string assignee treated as unassigned; claim-command spec covers takeover, if-available, and force paths.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: In-flight implementation (uncommitted, 2026-07-04): claim heldByOther now treats empty/whitespace assignee as unheld; new already_claimed_by CONFLICT error with why + nextSteps (pm next / --if-available / coordinated --force) so claim is atomic test-and-set instead of silent takeover; silent-steal path now only reachable with explicit --force (warning claim_takeover:prev->new). claim-command.spec updated for the new conflict contract.

patch.value:
## Context
Filed by the 2026-07-03 concurrency audit: `pm claim <id>` performed a silent takeover when the item was already assigned to another agent — two parallel agents could both believe they own the same item, breaking multi-agent work distribution. Only a warning (`claim_takeover`) was emitted, which agents don't reliably read.

## Design (implemented, uncommitted as of 2026-07-04)
- Claim is now an atomic test-and-set: if the item is held by a different author and neither `--force` nor `--if-available` is passed, claim throws a CONFLICT PmCliError with code `already_claimed_by`, naming the current holder.
- Error carries agent-actionable guidance: why (parallel agents must never proceed believing they co-own an item) and nextSteps — `pm next` to pick different work, `--if-available` for a no-op skip, `--force` only for coordinated takeover.
- `heldByOther` now treats empty/whitespace-only assignee as unheld (previously an empty-string assignee counted as "held", blocking claims on effectively unassigned items).
- Semantics matrix: unheld → claim succeeds; held + `--if-available` → skip with `claim_skipped_held_by` warning (exit 0); held + `--force` → takeover with `claim_takeover:prev->new` warning; held + neither → CONFLICT `already_claimed_by`.
- tests/unit/commands/claim-command.spec.ts updated for the new conflict contract.

## Constraints / decisions
- `--force` keeps the takeover path (needed for reclaiming from dead agents); the fix removes only the SILENT steal default.
- Terminal-item claim still requires `--force` (pre-existing rule, unchanged).
- The claim mutation itself runs under the item lock, so with pm-2muu's lock wait this is a true test-and-set (read assignee + write happen atomically w.r.t. other mutators).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Session handoff (2026-07-04, maintainer-agent): fix implemented in local working tree alongside pm-2muu (shared uncommitted diff), NOT committed yet. Body documents the full claim semantics matrix. Ships in the same PR as pm-2muu; verify the already_claimed_by conflict path in a two-agent sandbox before closing.

value.text: Evidence: claim takeover now rejects by default with already_claimed_by, --if-available skips held items, and --force records forced takeover. Verified by focused claim/integration tests, full coverage, and installed-package temp smoke.

Verification passed: claim-focused tests and installed-package temp smoke confirmed owner claim plus foreign --if-available skip.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Verification update: current-head review fix makes pm claim --if-available a true no-op when an item is held by another author. Regression now asserts skipped result preserves persisted updated_at and history length; focused claim test passed and full coverage passed 5380 tests at 100/100/100/100.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Sandbox verification on rebuilt dist (post-commit 010bb349), fresh init workspace: (1) agent-a claim -> success; (2) agent-b claim -> exit 4 CONFLICT code=already_claimed_by; (3) agent-b claim --if-available -> exit 0 no-op skip; (4) agent-b claim --force -> takeover, assignee=agent-b; (5) 6 parallel claims by distinct authors -> exactly 1 winner + 5 structured already_claimed_by conflicts, final assignee matches the single winner (pre-fix baseline: 3/6 got success payloads). Claim is now a true atomic test-and-set.

author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: pm claim on an item assigned to another agent fails atomically with CONFLICT already_claimed_by (naming the holder) unless --force; --if-available stays a no-op skip; empty-string assignee treated as unassigned; claiming your own item stays idempotent.

Shipped in commit 010bb349 (PR #430): claim is an atomic test-and-set under the item lock. Sandbox matrix on rebuilt dist: foreign claim exit 4 already_claimed_by; --if-available exit 0 skip; --force takeover; 6-way parallel race yields exactly 1 winner + 5 structured conflicts (was 3/6 false successes). claim-command unit + cli integration specs cover takeover/if-available/force paths.

message: Shipped and verified: atomic test-and-set claim ends silent steals; parallel race now yields exactly one owner.
